title: Tiny Sprite Classifier: Design the Augmentation Policy
strategies: no-augmentation baseline: return an empty policy; the 1-NN gallery is just the raw handful of sprites, reproducing the evaluator's weak reference (~0.1)

single-lever shift augmentation: notice the sprites are translated and add a few shifted copies so shifted test sprites find shifted gallery matches, capturing most of the shift gain but ignoring noise/brightness

matched multi-transform policy: augment with exactly the test-time nuisances (translation + Gaussian noise + brightness offset), spending most of the copy budget on the dominant translation and estimating magnitudes from the training sprites, while avoiding flips/contrast/cutout that the test set never contains

budget-aware tuning / search: read train_images to estimate the shift spread and background noise, sweep magnitudes and copy allocations under the total-copies cap, and keep the allocation that maximizes inferred held-out accuracy
